description: allocate_payment_io_buffers uses cudaMallocHost for page-locked memory so cudaMemcpyAsync is truly async; the other two allocators use malloc (acceptable because their callers use sync cudaMemcpy). Unifying onto malloc for all three re-introduces the checkout-latency regression.

findings.rationale: allocate_payment_io_buffers calls cudaMallocHost (returns cudaError_t, error checked via != cudaSuccess); the other two call malloc (returns void*, error checked via == NULL). Same statement count, same BRANCH count, but different callees and different comparison targets — same family as T045 (operator-label) and T051 (callee-label).
